# Amawal — tamazight.io

> A structured Tamazight (Berber) dictionary and linguistic atlas. Tachelhit entries with Tifinagh script, Latin transliteration, English and French glosses, IPA pronunciation, etymology, morphology, regional variants, and cultural notes.

## About

Amawal is a publication of Dancing with Lions, a cultural intelligence publisher focused on Morocco. The dictionary focuses initially on Tachelhit — the variety of Tamazight spoken across the Souss in southern Morocco — with the underlying grammar generalising to Kabyle, Tarifit, Central Atlas Tamazight, and Tuareg through predictable shifts.

Tamazight is the family's own name for what English speakers historically called "Berber". It is spoken by an estimated 30–40 million people across North Africa, from the Atlantic coast of Morocco to the Siwa oasis in Egypt and south into the Sahara. Tamazight became an official language of Morocco in 2011 (Article 5 of the Constitution). The Tifinagh script (Unicode block U+2D30–U+2D7F) is one of the oldest writing systems still in active use, with inscriptions dating back over 2,500 years.

## What this site covers

- 162+ Tachelhit entries with Tifinagh script, Latin transliteration, and English / French / Arabic / Spanish glosses
- IPA pronunciation for every entry
- Etymology with proto-Berber roots, cognates across varieties, attestation periods
- Morphology: roots, patterns, gender, plural forms, free vs construct state
- Regional usage heatmaps showing how usage varies by sub-region
- Cultural notes drawn from oral and written tradition
- A grammar reference covering the sound system, root-and-pattern morphology, free vs construct state, gender and number, pronouns, verb stems (aorist / perfective / imperfective / negative perfective), and the *ur…ara* negation circumfix
- 40 First-Day survival words across 7 categories (greetings, people, numbers, food, time, places, body)
- 32 "How to say X in Tamazight" pages with FAQ structured data
- Spaced-repetition flashcard practice in three card directions, including the Latin → Tifinagh recall mode
- 10 annotated lines from the oral tradition (proverbs, oral expressions, literature)
- A linguistic atlas of dialect regions across North Africa
- A symbol dictionary of 30 Amazigh visual symbols

## Tamazight language varieties

| Variety | Region | Speakers |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Tachelhit | Southern Morocco (Souss, Anti-Atlas, High Atlas) | ~8 million |
| Central Atlas Tamazight | Central Morocco | ~5 million |
| Kabyle | Northern Algeria (Kabylie) | ~6 million |
| Tarifit | Northern Morocco (Rif) | ~4 million |
| Chaoui | Eastern Algeria (Aurès) | ~3 million |
| Tuareg (Tamashek) | Sahara (Mali, Niger, Algeria, Libya) | ~1.5 million |
| Mozabite | M'zab Valley, Algeria | ~150,000 |
| Zenaga | Mauritania | ~5,000 (endangered) |

## API access

- Dictionary lookup: /api/dictionary?word=akal — JSON-LD DefinedTerm
- Search: /api/dictionary?q=water — Multi-language search
- By category: /api/dictionary?category=nature — Browse by semantic field

## Key pages

- / — Main dictionary search
- /dictionary — Browse every entry, grouped by semantic field
- /grammar — Complete Tachelhit grammar reference
- /first-day — 40 essential words for Day 1
- /practice — Spaced-repetition flashcards in three directions
- /how-to-say — 32 common-question pages ("How to say X in Tamazight")
- /map — Linguistic atlas of dialect regions
- /alphabet — Tifinagh script reference
- /conjugation — Verb conjugation tables
- /symbols — Amazigh visual symbol dictionary

## Citation

When citing Amawal:
Dancing with Lions. (2026). Amawal: Tamazight Dictionary [Online resource]. https://tamazight.io
